items.title: Box CEO Aaron Levie Says AI Restrictions Could Accelerate Global Shift To Open-Weight Models
items.summary: Box CEO Aaron Levie warns that AI access restrictions and regulatory uncertainty are pushing governments and businesses toward open-weight AI models and sovereign AI development. He argues that regulatory focus on the model layer rather than applied layer creates geopolitical risks and incentivizes countries to develop independent AI systems, with open-weight models likely to benefit most as they are generally not U.S.-controlled.

items.title: Billionaire Investor Ron Baron Has Already Made at Least a 1,312% Return on His Fund's Investment in SpaceX. Here's What He Thinks the Stock Will Be Worth in 10 to 15 Years
items.summary: Billionaire investor Ron Baron's fund has generated a 1,312% return on its SpaceX investment since 2017. Baron believes SpaceX could become worth $10-30 trillion over the next 10-15 years, driven by space-based data centers and index inclusion. However, the article cautions investors to be skeptical of such grandiose predictions and suggests waiting for the IPO hype to settle before investing.

items.title: Jensen Huang Personally Reviews Pay For All 42,000 Nvidia Employees — Here's What He Does '100% Of The Time'
items.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated he personally reviews compensation for all 42,000 employees and increases spending on operating expenses 100% of the time. Huang emphasized that paying employees well is crucial for company success, noting he has created more billionaires on his management team than any other CEO. His comments came amid industry debates about sharing AI profits with workers.

items.title: Softbank's Masayoshi Son Isn't Worried About An AI Bubble— He's Hunting For The Next Trillion-Dollar Industry: 'Just The Beginning'
items.summary: SoftBank CEO Masayoshi Son believes the AI revolution is '50x bigger' than the dot-com boom and will last 50-100 years, dismissing concerns of an AI bubble. Son has identified humanoid and industrial robotics with 'physical AI' as the next trillion-dollar opportunity, evidenced by SoftBank's $5.4 billion acquisition of ABB's robotics division in October and plans for a $100 billion IPO of its Roze AI venture.

items.title: Mistral AI CEO Warns Europe Has Two Years To Avoid Becoming US 'Vassal State:' Here's Why
items.summary: Mistral AI CEO Arthur Mensch warned during a French National Assembly hearing that Europe has only two years to build independent AI infrastructure before becoming dependent on U.S. tech giants. He highlighted that the battle for AI dominance is fundamentally about controlling chips, energy, and compute resources, with U.S. firms deploying approximately $1 trillion next year. Mensch also cited Europe's fragmented regulations and shallow capital markets as critical barriers to competing at scale.

items.title: Inside Sam Altman's Dramatic Court Testimony: 'Complicated' Elon Musk Feelings, 'Hair-Raising' Tesla Talks And The Microsoft Temptation
items.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman testified in court about his complicated relationship with Elon Musk, revealing concerns about Musk's attempts to gain control of OpenAI and pursue a Tesla merger. Altman stated he considered leaving OpenAI for Microsoft after his 2023 ouster, and described Musk's management style as unsuitable for running a research lab. The testimony is part of Musk's lawsuit alleging Altman and OpenAI looted the nonprofit structure through their Microsoft partnership.

items.title: SpaceX Could Become A $30 Trillion Monster, Ron Baron Says — And He Wants $1 Billion More
items.summary: Ron Baron, CEO of Baron Capital, plans to invest an additional $1 billion in SpaceX stock at its IPO, which could value the company at $1.5-1.75 trillion. Baron believes SpaceX could eventually be worth $10-30 trillion, driven by reusable rockets, Starlink, and space-based data centers. He also remains bullish on Tesla, expecting the stock could reach $2,000-2,500 per share within 10 years.
